architecture_fit.note: No transformer at all — model_fn hand-writes the softmax output array directly, not a delta from base_model.py, so it never tests whether a model's attention respects boundaries.

baseline_comparison.note: Leans on the framework's built-in uniform baseline (sharpness 0, lift +0.94) but constructs no additional strawman of its own.

faithfulness.note: No model exists to ablate; attention is the answer written by hand, and the README neither flags the faithfulness gap nor proposes a causal check.

operating_range.note: Only the canonical seed=0 config is run; fixed eps values, no variation across input scale or any other axis.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-set and reproduces the target masses, but it hardcodes the distribution itself rather than QK weights that yield it through softmax — the degenerate form of a hand-built circuit.

visual_judgement.note: App boots structurally, but all three Demo plots render empty: charts read benchmark.get('sweep') while sweep is nested under payload, and the heatmap needs attention_weights.npy that main.py never saves.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'Why this visualisation' section is articulate and maps charts to payload fields and the goal, though it describes views that do not actually render.

notes: A pure hand-written attention array that hardcodes the desired region masses directly, so it scores the headline metric trivially while never engaging the goal's real question of whether a model's attention respects boundaries. There is no transformer, no ablation, and no operating-range exploration, making faithfulness and architecture fit the binding failures. The Demo visualisation is also non-functional because the plotting code reads keys at the wrong nesting level and the heatmap artefact is never saved.
